site.title: Digital Security Checklists for Activists
site.description: Plain language steps for digital security. Because protecting yourself helps keep your whole community safer.

essentials.title: Security essentials
essentials.description: Core security practices every activist should follow.

signal.description: Secure your Signal messaging app for safer communications.

protest.description: Digital security for anyone attending a protest.

ice.description: Digital security for ICE watch and rapid response networks.

doxxing.title: Doxxing defense
doxxing.description: Protect yourself from doxxing and online harassment.

travel.title: Travel & flight security
travel.description: Digital security practices for activists traveling.

emergency.title: Emergency planning
emergency.description: Establish an emergency support network in case you're detained or threatened.

secondary.title: Secondary phone
secondary.description: Set up an extra phone for activism and protests.

hero.title: Digital Security Checklists for Activists
hero.description: Plain language steps for digital security. Because protecting yourself helps keep your whole community safer.

trustPoints.experienceDescription: Our guides draw from real organizing work and direct feedback from activists. While every situation is unique, we aim to share practical insights that can help inform your decisions.
trustPoints.updatedDescription: Our content is regularly reviewed by organizers who understand security concerns. We work to keep information current as risks and technologies evolve.

misconceptions.sectionDescription: When we start talking about digital security, we often hear these concerns. We think there's more to the story.
misconceptions.nothingToHideDescription: The more engaged you are in any social movement work, the more your data can be used to map networks of activists. The best time to start protecting yourself is before you're as deeply involved.
misconceptions.alreadyKnowDescription: While data collection is widespread, you can significantly reduce your digital footprint and protect future activities.
misconceptions.dontCareTitle: "They don't care about what I'm doing"
misconceptions.dontCareDescription: Mass surveillance affects everyone. Your data can be used to profile communities, predict protests, or target those you care about. Protecting your communications helps protect all of us.

homepage.trustHeading: Simple guides to keep us more safe
homepage.trustDescription: We built this because digital security shouldn't be overwhelming. We take a harm reduction approach: start where you are and do what you can.

translationFallback.message: This page is not yet available in your language. You are viewing the English version.
translationFallback.suggest: You can use your browser's built-in translation feature to translate this page.

branding.title: Activist Checklist
branding.description: Plain language steps for digital security, because protecting yourself helps keep your whole community safer.

footer.disclaimer: Heads up: We provide info and resources, not legal advice. Consult an attorney for your specific situation.

riskLevel.everyoneDefault: This section is for anyone doing activism or advocacy work.
riskLevel.mediumDefault: This section is for you if you are in a leadership role or you are doing activism that is more likely be targetted by the state or your opposition.
riskLevel.highDefault: This section is for you are in a high-profile role or your activism involves high-risk work that could result in serious consequences or retaliation.
riskLevel.forYouIf: This section is for you if:

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: Your feedback helps make this resource more useful and secure for everyone. We welcome suggestions for:
contact.suggestion1: Correcting any inaccuracies or outdated information
contact.suggestion2: Making our guides clearer and easier to understand
contact.suggestion4: Suggesting new topics we should cover
contact.successMessage: Message sent successfully! We'll get back to you soon.
contact.githubDescription: If you're okay with sharing publicly, you can submit feedback, questions, and feature requests through our GitHub repository.
contact.emailDescription: Using Proton Mail? Your messages will be automatically encrypted. For other providers, you can use our PGP key. Or you can just email this address unencrypted.

contactForm.emailNote: Note on email replies: Unless you use Proton Mail, your email provider can read any replies we send to your message.
contactForm.signalUsernameHelp: Your username is different than your display name. Find your username at Signal > User Icon (top right) > Settings. It will be listed near the top, just underneath your phone number.
contactForm.privacyText: Your message is securely encrypted and sent to our Proton Mail account. We don't collect any personal information about you.
contactForm.errorMessage: Failed to send message. Please try again later.
contactForm.responseNone: No response needed (your message remains anonymous)

feedbackCta.heading: Have Questions?
feedbackCta.description: Let us know if you have questions or feedback so we can make these guides as useful as possible.
feedbackCta.successMessage: Thanks for your feedback! We'll review it and use it to improve the guides. If you're finding the site valuable, please help spread the word!

news.title: Surveillance News
news.description: Latest news about state surveillance and threats facing social movements.
news.tipInvitation: Have news you think should be included?
news.tipSuffix: and help keep our community informed.

changelog.title: Recent site updates
